channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Israel and Lebanon ceasefire extended: White House
item.description: The ceasefire between Hezbollah and Israel, announced by former President Biden in November, is now set “to be in effect until February 18, 2025,” the statement reads.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Trump’s Palestinian refugee idea falls flat with Arab allies and confounds a Republican senator
item.description: DORAL, Fla. (AP) — President Donald Trump’s push to have Egypt and Jordan take in large numbers of Palestinian refugees from besieged Gaza fell flat with those countries' governments and left a key congressional ally in Washington perplexed on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's Q&A on Air Force One goes from the plane's color scheme to the fate of TikTok and Canada
item.description: ABOARD AIR FORCE ONE (AP) — President Donald Trump hasn’t been shy about sharing his thoughts since taking office, and he added a 20-minute Q&A with reporters aboard Air Force One to the mix.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: List: All of Trump’s executive orders on the economy
item.description: President Donald Trump started his second administration with a flurry of policy actions since returning to the White House on Jan. 20.
item.dc:creator: Sean Noone, NewsNation

item.title: Alaskans say Trump can change the name of Denali but can't make people call it Mount McKinley
item.description: President Donald Trump has changed the name of Alaska's Denali back to Mount McKinley. It's a nod to President William McKinley, who was from Ohio and who never set foot in Alaska. Many Alaskans say they'll never stop calling the mountain Denali.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Colombian leader quickly caves after Trump threats, offers presidential plane for deportation flights
item.description: Gustavo Petro was quickly brought to heel by President Donald Trump after the Colombian leader rejected several deportation flights from the U.S. Colombia has agreed to accept deported migrants.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sen. Marshall 'absolutely' comfortable with Trump’s purge of inspectors general
item.description: Sen. Roger Marshall (R-Kan.) suggested that, like Reagan, Trump could potentially rehire some of the watchdog officials after cleaning house at the agencies.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Trump should rethink revoking former officials' security details, Tom Cotton says
item.description: A top Republican Senate ally says that President Donald Trump should rethink his decision to remove security details from three former senior national security officials, saying their protection is not just for them but also the public.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Beaumont, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Graham says Republicans may 'own another attack' on US if Trump border czar doesn't get deportation funds fast
item.description: Sen. Lindsey Graham, R-S.C., said congressional Republicans who delay funding for border czar Tom Homan could "own another attack on our country.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: White House says Colombia agrees to take deported migrants after Trump tariff showdown
item.description: The White House is claiming victory in a showdown with Colombia over accepting flights of deported migrants from the U.S., hours after President Donald Trump threatened steep tariffs on imports and other sanctions on the longtime U.S. partner.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Ramaswamy's expected run for Ohio governor would test experienced Republicans and tradition
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — Fresh off of his unexpected departure from President Donald Trump's Department of Government Efficiency, Vivek Ramaswamy has set his sights on becoming governor of his home state.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH and THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Senate tees up final vote on Bessent nomination to lead Treasury
item.description: The Senate on Saturday voted to advance Scott Bessent’s nomination to become the next secretary of the Treasury, putting him in line to be confirmed on Monday. Senators voted 67-23 to tee up final passage on Bessent’s nomination. The final vote will take place Monday evening at 5:30 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Conservatives of color have lofty expectations for Trump's second term
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Delivering his first address as a reinaugurated president, Donald Trump spoke directly to communities that had historically shunned his party.
item.dc:creator: MATT BROWN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democratic states weigh more support for immigrants as Trump administration cracks down
item.description: The resistance efforts in California, New York and other states are a counterpoint to the many Republican-led states.
item.dc:creator: DAVID A. LIEB, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: In policy reversal, Trump eliminates help for Black and Latino communities hit harder by pollution
item.description: President Trump is reversing his predecessor’s efforts to clean up areas hardest hit by pollution from heavy industry, ports and roadways – communities that are often largely Black, Latino and low-income.
item.dc:creator: Michael Phillis And Alexa St. John, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump ‘very surprised’ by Murkowski, Collins votes against Hegseth
item.description: President Donald Trump said Friday that he wasn't expecting Republican Sens. Lisa Murkowski (Alaska) and Susan Collins (Maine) to vote against his controversial nominee for secretary of Defense, Army veteran and former Fox News host Pete Hegseth.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Measure to make California an independent country cleared to gather signatures
item.description: Another ballot measure asking whether California should be its own country is cleared to start gathering signatures, the California Secretary of State announced Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Republican proposes amendment to allow Trump to serve third term
item.description: Like other U.S. presidents, Trump is barred from running for a third White House term by the 22nd Amendment to the Constitution.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump has tossed a lot of Biden policies. But there’s one AI measure he wants to keep
item.description: President Trump says he likes Biden’s idea to open up federal lands for AI data centers. His White House is looking for ways to ensure U.S. dominance in the sector.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram, MPR News

item.title: Elon Musk tried to turn the salute controversy into a joke
item.description: The incident only lasted seconds, but it sparked what has become a global debate about how to interpret what Musk did. Then Musk started posting Nazi-themed jokes.
item.dc:creator: Jude Joffe-Block and Shannon Bond, MPR News

item.title: Trump signs order directing release of JFK assassination files
item.description: The order directs the director of national intelligence and attorney general to present a plan within 15 days for the “full and complete release of records relating to the assassination of President John F. Kennedy.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Walz proposes $887 million bonding bill for infrastructure projects, not worried about Capitol impasse
item.description: Using the breached Rapidan Dam as a backdrop Gov. Tim Walz pitched his $887 million bonding bill. As well as funding for infrastructure, Walz wants to fund a new state patrol headquarters, and address housing and environmental needs.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Yang, MPR News

item.title: Nevada state senator takes on gender, sports issue at UNLV, UNR, community colleges
item.description: Republican state Senator Carrie Buck is sponsoring legislation that would require Nevada colleges and universities to restrict participation in athletics based on the athlete's gender at birth.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Sen. Lisa Murkowski to vote against Hegseth; first Republican to oppose a Trump Cabinet pick
item.description: Sen. Lisa Murkowski announced Thursday that she will vote against confirming Pete Hegseth to lead Pentagon, becoming the first Republican to oppose one of President Donald Trump's Cabinet picks.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Federal judge temporarily blocks Trump’s executive order ending birthright citizenship
item.description: A federal judge on Thursday temporarily blocked President Donald Trump’s executive order ending the constitutional guarantee of birthright citizenship regardless of the parents’ immigration status.
item.dc:creator: EUGENE JOHNSON and MIKE CATALINI Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: St. Paul City Council approves charter change to allow administrative fines
item.description: The St. Paul City Council on Wednesday voted to amend the city’s charter to allow it to impose fines for ordinance violations. It was one of few large cities in Minnesota that didn’t already have that power.
item.dc:creator: Estelle Timar-Wilcox, MPR News

item.title: NJ bill looks to eliminate hurdle for student service members
item.description: FLORHAM PARK, N.J. (PIX11) - From studying to serving: that was National Guard veteran Frank "Doc" Schupp's life in the late 2000s. He was called to deploy to Iraq at the end of his last semester of his junior year at Kean University, in the midst of prepping for finals.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: GOP leaders stare down legislative minefield after Trump's Day 1 blitz
item.description: President Trump made a big splash with dozens of executive actions on his first day in office, but now comes the hard part: getting Republicans in Congress in line to usher an ambitious legislative agenda through their slim majorities and clashing factions. Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, Fox 5

item.title: Gen. Milley portrait taken down from Pentagon
item.description: The portrait, which had just been unveiled Jan. 10, hung in the Joint Chiefs hallway next to those of other former chairs.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, Fox 5

item.title: What exactly is an executive order? Trump's tool for quickly reshaping government
item.description: Donald Trump returned to the White House ready to immediately overhaul the government using the fastest tool he has — the executive order.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Here are the biggest fights Democrats are bracing for in Trump's second term
item.description: House Democrats are bracing for major fights in the coming year as President Trump enters the White House for his second term and Republicans control all levers of power in Washington.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, Fox 5

item.title: NJ governor sends letter to Trump seeking to end congestion pricing
item.description: NEW JERSEY (PIX11) – New Jersey Gov. Phil Murphy sent a letter to President Donald Trump on Monday seeking to end congestion pricing in New York City. Congestion pricing went into effect on Jan. 5.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11
